title: Parson Tower Desk
summary: A desk built from two identical shelf towers with a shared plywood-and-2x2-trim top screwed on from below — no glue in final assembly, so it comes apart to move.
description:
This desk splits into three simple assemblies: two shelf towers, built as separate identical units, and a tabletop built on the bench before either tower is involved. Building the towers as standalone shelf units first, complete with their own 2x2 trim, means most of the joinery happens flat on a bench rather than fighting gravity against an assembled desk.

The tabletop is genuinely simple — 2x2 trim wraps the underside of a ripped plywood or MDF top, attached flush to the bottom edge, with the end trim going on first and the long front/back trim second. Keeping bottom edges flush throughout is the only real technique this step needs.

**Final assembly is deliberately screw-only, no glue** — laying the finished top onto the two towers and driving screws up through the tower's pocket holes into the underside of the top. That's a specific choice to keep the desk able to come apart for a move. One practical note carried over from the build itself: the towers may not be perfectly square after their own assembly, so you may need to rotate one slightly to get a clean fit under the top before driving the final screws.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood/MDF top and shelves to width.

materials.name: 3/4" MDF or plywood, full sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-1/2" widths for the tabletop and shelves.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one desktop at 57" and four tower shelves at 15" from the 3/4" MDF or plywood, full sheet. Cut ten shelf trim, perpendicular to desktop at 15-1/2", eight shelf trim, parallel to desktop at 15", eight legs at 28-1/2" and two desktop side trim at 60" from the 2x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to seven 8 ft 2x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the shelf trim assemblies
steps.body: With the tops flush, glue and screw the shelves to the perpendicular and parallel trim pieces using 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Build four of these — two per tower.

steps.title: Build the tower legs
steps.body: Mark the legs for the center shelf position, then glue and screw through the pocket holes into the legs, attaching the shelf trim assemblies. Each leg's top gets a single pocket hole for attaching the desktop later. Build two towers.

steps.title: Build the tabletop
steps.body: Attach the end trim to the desktop first, keeping bottom edges flush. Then attach the front and back (long) trim pieces to the top and the end trim, same flush-bottom rule.

steps.title: Final assembly
steps.body: Lay the finished desktop on top of the two towers, screw-only with no glue so the desk can be taken apart to move. Drive screws through the towers' pocket holes into the underside of the desktop. The towers may not be perfectly square — rotate as needed for a clean fit before final tightening.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces smooth, then prime and paint.

unresolvedImages.alt: Desk built from two shelf towers with a shared plywood top
